intro: Acronia penelops, also known as the "forest elf," is a rare and endangered plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Acronia penelops include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Acronia penelops is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or extract, and is often used to treat fever, pain, and digestive issues.
